events.text: Jeffrey Wilson, 63-year-old White male, Current smoker (1 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the Sigmoid colon. Clinical staging: T4aN0M0, AJCC Stage IIA. ICD-10: C18.9. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent surgical resection: Laparoscopic left hemicolectomy with lymph node dissection (22 nodes examined, 0/22 positive). Estimated blood loss 200 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 1 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: FOLFOX (5-FU/Leucovorin/Oxaliplatin) q2w x 12 cycles. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after adjuvant chemotherapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Wilson
MRN: patient_9bb40ad67699
Date of Study: 2022-11-04

CLINICAL INDICATION: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Findings consistent with moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma as described.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Wilson
MRN: patient_9bb40ad67699
Specimen ID: S2022-46697
Date of Procedure: 2022-11-08
Date of Report: 2022-11-10

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 7.3 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Wilson - Sigmoid colon" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 7.3 x 5.1 x 3.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A4.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Jeffrey Wilson
MRN: patient_9bb40ad67699
Date: 2022-11-11
Provider: Dr. Priya Sharma

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Wilson is a 63-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, IIA. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy. Pathology confirmed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- coronary artery disease

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Engineer
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of cancer.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dry mouth, mild cough. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 114/87, HR 71, RR 18, Temp 98.6F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 77.5 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.2, Hgb 10.1, Plt 154, Cr 0.81

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
63-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. initial oncology consultation.

1. Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, Stage IIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend surgical resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Wilson
MRN: patient_9bb40ad67699
Date of Report: 2022-11-19
Specimen: Sigmoid colon (Resection)
Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 5.6 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS Wild-type - No mutation [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R175H - Missense (Exon 5)
  APC p.R1450* (c.4348C>T) - Nonsense (Exon 16)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  SMAD4 p.R361C - VUS
  FBXW7 p.R465H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS wild-type: Patient eligible for anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) if disease progresses.
  - MSS: Unlikely to benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 796x coverage.
Tumor content: 52%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Jeffrey Wilson
MRN: patient_9bb40ad67699
Specimen ID: S2022-79236
Date of Procedure: 2022-12-09
Date of Report: 2022-12-13

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.4 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Jeffrey Wilson - Sigmoid colon" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.4 x 4.5 x 3.2 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.7, Hgb 12.8, Plt 204, Cr 0.82

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
63-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. postoperative follow-up.

1. Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma - postoperative follow-up
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Monitoring for treatment-related toxicity
2. Symptom management
   - Continue antiemetic regimen
3. Follow-up in 3 weeks

scenario_blurb: Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy
